content.problemStatement:
Let fff be a permutation of length NNN, where NNN is **even**. The riffle of fff is defined to be the permutation
g=(f(1),f(3),…,f(N−1),f(2),f(4),…,f(N))g = (f(1), f(3), \ldots, f(N-1), f(2), f(4), \ldots, f(N))g=(f(1),f(3),…,f(N−1),f(2),f(4),…,f(N))

You are given two integers NNN and KKK. Output the resultant permutation when you riffle the identity permutation of length NNN, KKK times.

The identity permutation of length NNN is σN=(1,2,…,N){\sigma}_N = (1, 2, \ldots, N)σN​=(1,2,…,N)


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer permutation as NNN space-separated integers in a new line.
constraints: The sum of NNN across test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
**Subtask 1 (30 points):** NNN is a power of 222
